title: nasturtium sylvestre subsp. barbaraeoides
intro: Nasturtium sylvestre subsp. barbaraeoides, also known as the desert nasturtium or western marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of glucosinolates. The plant has several health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Nasturtium sylvestre subsp. barbaraeoides can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
